response.justification: Strong domain expertise in geospatial systems and proven ability to build distributed, high-performance platforms, but significant technical skill gap. C++ experience dates to 2015 (11 years ago), and no Rust is mentioned—both are primary requirements for this role. Recent career trajectory is TypeScript/Node.js web engineering, not systems-level programming. Candidate's learning agility and seniority level suggest potential for ramp-up, but the gap is substantial enough to warrant careful evaluation of commitment to low-level systems work.
strengths: Extensive geospatial domain knowledge with real-world GIS platform development (Green Map, GISCollective) and successful FOSS4G presentations
Proven ability to build distributed systems at scale—handled millions of HTTP requests/sec with low-latency optimizations at Dunnhumby
Staff-level seniority with consistent ability to pick up diverse technologies across 15+ years (DLang, Java, C++, Node.js, etc.)
concerns: C++ expertise has significant recency gap (last relevant work in 2015); no recent systems programming or performance-critical development mentioned
No Rust experience listed anywhere on resume, despite it being an acceptable primary alternative to C++
Recent 7+ years focused exclusively on web/cloud stack (TypeScript, Node.js, React, MongoDB); no evidence of routing systems, tile-based data processing, or navigation guidance solutions
